channel.title: NYT > Top Stories
channel.copyright: Copyright 2026 The New York Times Company

item.title: As Trump Politicizes Justice Dept., Prosecutors Struggle With Grand Juries
item.description: Judges and grand juries have increasingly lost faith in the Justice Department as the president uses it to reward his friends and go after his opponents.
item.dc:creator: Alan Feuer
item.media:credit: Eric Lee for The New York Times
item.media:description: The examples of grand jury malfeasance come on top of the many cases in which Justice Department prosecutors have failed to get grand jurors to return indictments.

item.title: UFC Unveils Plans for White House Fight on Trump’s Birthday
item.description: The Ultimate Fighting Championship bout is scheduled for June 14, President Trump’s 80th birthday.
item.dc:creator: Luke Broadwater
item.media:description: Construction on the South Lawn of the White House on Monday, ahead of events for the United States’ 250th anniversary that include the upcoming UFC fight.

item.title: Trump Declares Himself in Good Health After Physical Exam at Walter Reed
item.description: President Trump, who will turn 80 next month, did not give details on the exam.
item.dc:creator: Karoun Demirjian
item.category: Reed, Walter, National Military Medical Center
item.media:description: President Trump has been observed with swelling in his legs, which the president’s doctors and aides said was a result of chronic venous insufficiency.

item.title: The Wars in Ukraine and Iran Are More Alike Than You May Think
item.description: Aspects such as drone technology and diplomacy show how the wars intersect on the battlefield and in global alignments, providing a model for future conflicts.
item.dc:creator: Lara Jakes
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:description: Assessing the damaged buildings following Russian strikes in Ukraine’s capital, Kyiv, on Sunday.

item.title: Iran Begins Lifting Monthslong Internet Blackout, Officials Say
item.description: Organizations that track online traffic confirmed an uptick in activity on Tuesday, as Iranians reconnected to the global internet after a government-imposed shutdown.
item.dc:creator: Yeganeh Torbati and Sanam Mahoozi
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Arash Khamooshi/Polaris for The New York Times
item.media:description: A woman waves an Iranian flag in Tehran, Iran. The authorities imposed an internet blackout during the war with the United States and Israel.

item.title: To Understand Pope Leo’s Efforts on A.I., Look at the Man Shaking His Hand
item.description: The co-founder of a prominent A.I. company was nearby as the pope implored A.I. leaders to slow down and consider the technology’s possible perils.
item.dc:creator: Elizabeth Dias
item.media:description: Pope Leo XIV shook the hand of Christopher Olah, a co-founder of the A.I. company Anthropic, at the presentation of the encyclical titled “Magnifica Humanitas,” or “Magnificent Humanity,” on Monday at the Vatican.

item.title: Scientists Ditched a Scary Climate Scenario. What Now?
item.description: While global warming is still a threat, the decision to back away from a worst-case outlook raises questions about whether some risks have been overstated.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer and Eric Niiler
item.media:description: A wildfire burning in the Simi Valley of California this month.

item.title: A Spring Heat Wave is Breaking Records in the U.K., Spain and France
item.description: Authorities in Britain and France warned that “unprecedented” temperatures — at a time when few expected them — could persist. Climate change has made heat waves more frequent and more intense.
item.dc:creator: Lynsey Chutel and Nazaneen Ghaffar
item.media:description: Temperatures in London climbed to 34.8 degrees Celsius, or nearly 95 degrees Fahrenheit, on Monday.

item.title: The Homesteading Mother of 6 Taking On Big Tech
item.description: Kassi Solberg has concerns about a proposed data center complex, the size of 3,800 football fields, near her home. Trust us, the developer says.
item.dc:creator: Juliet Macur and Will Warasila
item.media:description: Ms. Solberg arriving at a Yellowstone County hearing.

item.title: South Carolina Senate Does Not Act on New Midterm Map, Defying Trump
item.description: With early voting already underway, it became clear there was no longer enough support to use new lines before the November midterm elections.
item.dc:creator: Eduardo Medina, Emily Cochrane and Nick Corasaniti
item.media:credit: Jeffrey Collins/Associated Press, via Ap Photo/Jeffrey Collins
item.media:description: Members of the South Carolina House of Representatives at the State Capitol in Columbia this month.

item.title: Court Rejects Alabama House Map, Calling It Unfair to Black Voters
item.description: Alabama is likely to appeal the ruling, which stops an effort to use a new congressional map that would likely cost Democrats a majority-Black district.
item.dc:creator: Emily Cochrane and Abbie VanSickle
item.media:credit: Wes Frazer for The New York Times
item.media:description: People march for voting rights on the famed Edmund Pettus Bridge in Selma, Ala., in May.

item.title: U.S. Aims to Give Cold War Plutonium to Start-Ups for Nuclear Fuel
item.description: Companies say it’s a better way to dispose of the Cold War-era material — and fix a shortage of nuclear fuel. But the plan has also faced criticism.
item.dc:creator: Brad Plumer
item.category: Arms Control and Limitation and Disarmament
item.media:description: Work with radioactive materials at a plutonium facility at the at Los Alamos National Laboratory in 1978.

item.title: G.D.P. Is a Flawed Measure of Prosperity. Alternatives Are On the Way.
item.description: The United Nations has a new plan for weighing economic gains alongside health and environmental progress. But consensus is elusive.
item.dc:creator: Lydia DePillis

item.title: Supreme Court Reverses Ruling in Immigration Judges’ Free Speech Lawsuit
item.description: A group of immigration judges in 2020 challenged work-related restrictions on their public speaking engagements, saying they violated their free speech rights.
item.dc:creator: Ann E. Marimow
item.media:description: The justices of the Supreme Court said the U.S. Court of Appeals for the Fourth Circuit had overstepped in its ruling. There were no noted dissents.

item.title: Knicks Fans Celebrate the Team’s First NBA Finals Appearance Since 1999
item.description: After a 27-year drought, the New York Knicks are headed to the N.B.A. finals. Fans flooded the streets of the city in celebration.
item.dc:creator: David Waldstein and Vincent Alban
item.media:credit: Vincent Alban for The New York Times
item.media:description: The scenes in Midtown on Monday night after the Knicks won their playoff series against the Cavaliers.

item.title: ‘Bing Bong!’ The Knicks’ Watch Party Outside the Garden Is Dead.
item.description: The police say that celebrations outside of Madison Square Garden have gotten out of control. Fans and insiders say the chaos has been driven by social media influencers.
item.dc:creator: Jonah E. Bromwich, Chelsia Rose Marcius and Debra Kamin
item.media:credit: José A. Alvarado Jr. for The New York Times
item.media:description: Fans have been celebrating outside Madison Square Garden as the Knicks competed to reach the N.B.A. finals. But the celebrations have grown rowdy, and the city has decided to cancel the crowd-watching parties.

item.title: SpaceX IPO Filing Reveals Favorable Terms for Elon Musk
item.description: The ways it set up its board and Mr. Musk’s pay appear to benefit him at the expense of other shareholders, corporate governance experts said.
item.dc:creator: Ryan Mac
item.media:description: Central to Elon Musk’s power over SpaceX is his control of “super voting” shares.

item.title: The Average Guys Outsmarting Wall Street on Prediction Markets
item.description: How prediction-market ‘sharps’ have made millions wagering on everything from war to Rotten Tomatoes.
item.dc:creator: Adam Iscoe

item.title: Celebrity Assistants Exist to Indulge Their Bosses, but When Does Duty Cross a Line?
item.description: Matthew Perry’s assistant injected the ketamine that killed his employer. His sentencing has some in the demanding profession considering the power dynamics involved.
item.dc:creator: Matt Stevens
item.media:credit: Raymond Hall/GC Images, via Getty Images
item.media:description: An autopsy report attributed Matthew Perry’s death in 2023 to the “acute effects" of ketamine. The actor’s personal assistant had found him unresponsive in a hot tub in his home in Los Angeles.

item.title: ‘Armageddon’: The Blockbuster Melodrama That Left Our Heads Spinning
item.description: Michael Bay wasn’t remotely worried about the science in his 1998 science-fiction action hit that launched an elite ensemble cast to space.
item.dc:creator: Maya Salam

item.title: Deal or No Deal With Iran
item.description: A deal to end the current blockade is merely an enticement for the next blockade and the one after that.
item.dc:creator: Bret Stephens
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)

item.title: Your Questions, Answered
item.description: We respond to reader queries about weapons sales, the national debt and more.
item.dc:creator: Sam Sifton
item.category: US and Israeli Attack on Iran (2026)
item.media:credit: Saher Alghorra for The New York Times
item.media:description: In the Gaza Strip.

item.title: A Powerful H.I.V. Drug Lands in Zambia. But Will It Reach Those Who Need It?
item.description: Lenacapavir, which protects people from H.I.V. infection with twice-yearly shots, is arriving in a country where the health system has been hollowed out by American aid cuts.
item.dc:creator: Stephanie Nolen and Arlette Bashizi
item.category: Global Fund to Fight AIDS, Tuberculosis and Malaria
President's Emergency Plan for AIDS Relief
item.media:description: Health workers promoted a new H.I.V. prevention method at the University of Zambia in Lusaka in March and encouraged students to sign up to get it.

item.title: BP Ousts Chairman, Citing ‘Serious Concerns’ Over Conduct
item.description: The British oil group said that Albert Manifold had been removed, after concerns were raised “related to important governance standards, oversight and conduct.”
item.dc:creator: Gregory Schmidt
item.media:description: BP has gone through a series of leadership changes in recent years, amid investor discontent over its financial performance.

item.title: Iceland, Rattled by Trump’s Greenland Threats, Weighs Joining the E.U.
item.description: Iceland has stood apart from the rest of Europe. But President Trump’s threats to Greenland have provoked a reconsideration.
item.dc:creator: Amelia Nierenberg and Sergey Ponomarev
item.media:credit: Sergey Ponomarev for The New York Times
